data.title: MF flows in December: SIP growth doubles, equity flows pick up pace again, gold ETFs see a dip
data.description:
In December, mid-cap mutual funds recorded an inflow of Rs 5,093 crore, a 4 percent increase compared to November. Small-cap mutual funds saw a 13.5 percent increase in inflows, reaching Rs 4,667 crore in December.

The mutual fund industry ended 2024 on a positive note with assets under management (AUM) pegged at Rs 66.93 lakh crore, up 32 percent compared to 2023.

The equity segment continued to be a preferred choice for investors with equity-oriented funds recording inflows of around Rs 41,156 crore in December, the second highest in the year, just below October’s Rs 41,887 crore. The growth came despite continued market volatility.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

On the other hand, debt and fixed income funds saw significant outflows totalling a little over Rs 1.27 lakh crore. This was in sharp contrast to November when the category saw inflows of Rs 12,916 crore.

Himanshu Srivastava, Associate Director- Manager Research, Morningstar Investment Research India noted that the surge in the equity markets and the returns that equity oriented mutual funds have generated in the last few years have motivated several investors to enter the equity markets.

“It is pleasing to see more and more investors using mutual funds route to do so because of the obvious benefit that mutual fund offers. But a more mature behaviour is them not panicking during correction and rather utilising it to build exposure. However, it’s important for investors to have the right and reasonable expectation and not get swayed away by the quantum of returns generated in the past," he added.

Also read:Equity fund inflows jump 15% to Rs 41,156 cr in Dec, SIP book tops Rs 26,000 cr mark: AMFI

Here are some of the key equity highlights:

SIP growth doubles

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

For the month of December, the monthly contribution through SIP was Rs 26,459 crore, up from Rs 25,320 crore in November.  SIP AUM for the month was around Rs 13.63 lakh crore up from Rs 13.54 crore in November 2024.

Sanjay Bembalkar, Head - Equity at Union Asset Management Company Private Limited said that the trajectory of gross SIP flows is quite encouraging at 50.2 percent y-o-y increase. “These numbers indicate structural change in investing culture, thought process maturity and awareness among investors,” he added. This is evident in investor behaviour as seen in data compiled by Moneycontrol, which shows that investors have been continuing to invest in mutual funds despite market volatility, with an increasing percentage of investments coming on days of market lows.

Equity flows pick up pace again

For December, the total inflows into open-ended equity funds was around Rs 41,156 crore, up by around 14.5 percent from November 2024.  "The correction in the markets during the month provided a good investment opportunity for investors, which they didn’t fail to capitalise on. This logged the 46th consecutive month of net inflows into the segment," Srivastava noted.

While the number is lower than October 2024, Manish Mehta, national head, sales, marketing and digital business, Kotak Mutual Fund is optimistic. "We’ve returned to being more aligned with October’s numbers. November saw a dip, largely due to the festive season. During that period, investment typically takes a back seat as investors focus on festivities and spending. Looking at the monthly trends, it is clear that NFOs (New Fund Offers) have consistently supported the net numbers. For example, October was a strong month for NFOs, and December followed a similar trend," he explained adding that throughout the year, NFOs have been significant contributors to the mutual fund industry’s overall net figures.

Small and mid-cap funds continue to see demand

Amongst categories, small- and mid-cap funds continued to emerge as winners.  In December, mid-cap mutual funds recorded an inflow of Rs 5,093 crore, a 4 percent increase compared to November. Small-cap mutual funds saw a 13.5 percent increase in inflows, reaching Rs 4,667 crore in December. This comes despite continued concern on high valuations of small- and mid-cap space. Mehta notes that this is driven by continued investor preference, despite the risks.

"Over the past five to seven years, mutual funds have consistently highlighted the expensive valuations of small-cap and mid-cap categories, advising caution and favouring SIPs over lump sum investments. In the past year, many funds restricted lump sum investments in these segments, focusing on SIPs, which sustained steady inflows despite valuation concerns. However, investor returns in small-cap and mid-cap funds remain positive, reinforcing confidence," he explains adding that distributors report that investors view SIPs as a safe way to invest in these segments, which can be seen in the strong SIP numbers for the segment. "This strong SIP book ensures consistent monthly inflows of Rs 2,000 to Rs 4,000 crore, even as lump sum sales decline," he adds.

On the other hand large-cap mutual funds saw a decline of 21 percent, with inflows dropping from Rs 2,547 crore in November to Rs 2,010 crore in December 2024.

NFOs continue to drive thematic and sectoral funds

Sectoral and thematic funds continued to see demand with the maximum inflows in the equity category. For the month, thematic funds saw inflows worth Rs 15,332 crore against Rs 7,657 crore in November 2024. For the category, NFOs continue to be the main drivers. The inflows for the month includes around Rs 11,337 crore collected from 12 new sector or thematic NFOs launched in December.

Inflows into Gold ETFs decline

While for the month of December, gold ETF category continued to see inflows, on a comparative basis, the inflows into the segment for the month were lower than the Rs 1,257 crore seen in November.  Srivastava explains that the lesser quantum of net inflows could be due to investor shifting their focus towards equity markets. "That said, in absolute terms, a net inflow of Rs 640 crores is also meaningful. Gold prices also corrected which would have prompted some investors to continue their investments in gold ETFs," he explained.

In 2024, gold ETF category received a net inflow of Rs 11,226 crore up from just Rs 2,920 crore in 2023.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Capex cycle in India not yet on durable, self-sustaining path: Nomura
data.description:
Sectorally, new investment contracted on a y-o-y basis in FY25 ytd (April-December) for machinery, electricity, mining, transport services and infrastructure, while picking up for chemicals, metals, transport equipment, construction & real estate and IT. (Representational image)

New investments projects announced slowed in the quarter ending December 2024, suggesting that the capex cycle in India is not yet on a durable, self-sustaining path, according to a note issued by Nomura on Thursday.

In the note issued on January 9, Nomura's economists wrote, "Quarterly fluctuations aside, the size of new investment projects announced remains low by historical standards. This might reflect a mix of the recent slump in public capex, lacklustre domestic demand, and competition from Chinese imports.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"While public capex should improve in the coming quarters, we are concerned that India’s broader cyclical slowdown amid rising global uncertainties, may impede private investment appetite in the near term".

Also read: Budget 2025: Govt likely to ease conditions on capex loans to states to meet Rs 1.5 lakh cr target in FY25

The economists quoted the data from the Centre for Monitoring the Indian Economy (CMIE), which showed that the pace of new investment projects in Q4 2024 (ending December 2024) slowed to ~Rs6.2 trillion versus Rs 7.2 trillion a quarter ago (Q3 FY24 or quarter ending September 2024), and Rs 7.8 trillion a year ago (in Q4 2023 or quarter ending December 2023).

On a 4-quarter rolling sum basis, new investment stood at 9.7 percent of GDP in December 2024 quarter vs 10.5% in September 2024 quarter, and 13.3% of GDP in December 2023 quarter. New investment announcements in manufacturing were broadly stable at 4.7 percent of GDP, while in infrastructure they fell to 3.6 percent of GDP from ~4.2 percent in the previous two quarters, said the note.

Sectorally, new investment contracted on a y-o-y basis in FY25 ytd (April-December) for machinery, electricity, mining, transport services and infrastructure, while picking up for chemicals, metals, transport equipment, construction & real estate and IT.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Adani Wilmar's OFS for 20% stake opens January 10, floor price Rs 275 per share
data.description:

Volume
Todays L/H

Adani Wilmar has informed that one of the promoter companies, Adani Commodities will be selling up to 20% stake in the company through an Offer For Sale starting on January 10, with a floor price of Rs 275 per share.

The OFS will have a base offer of 13.5% equity and a green shoe option for another 6.5% stake, a company filing said on January 9.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

On December 30, 2024, Adani Enterprises has fully exited the Adani Wilmar JV, selling its 13% stake to meet the minimum public shareholding norm, with the remaining 31% stake going to Singapore's Wilmar International.

The divestment will raise proceeds worth over $2 billion which Adani Enterprises said would be used in 'core infrastructure platforms'.

This is being updated.

data.title: TCS promoted over 25,000 employees in Q3, nearly 20% of workforce elevated this fiscal
data.description:
Representative image

Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) promoted more than 25,000 employees in the third quarter of FY25, bringing the total promotions for the fiscal to nearly 20 percent, or over 1,10,000, of its workforce, India’s largest IT services firm said in a release on January 9.

“We promoted over 25,000 associates this quarter, which brought the total promotions this financial year to more than 110,000. We continue to invest in employee upskilling and overall well-being. Our campus hiring for the year is going according to plan and preparations are afoot to onboard a higher number of campus hires next year," chief human resources officer Milind Lakkad said.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Mumbai-headquartered firm also reported a net decline of 5,370 employees during the quarter, marking a reversal after two consecutive quarters of headcount growth.

TCS now employs 6,07,354 people, the company said, as it shared the numbers for the third quarter of FY25.

Attrition during the quarter rose marginally to 13 percent from 12.3 percent in the September quarter.

The IT behemoth said its employees clocked over 4 crore learning hours and acquired 38 lakh competencies year-to-date.

Chief executive officer and managing director K Krithivasan said the company’s continued investments in upskilling, artificial intelligence/generative AI (AI/Gen AI) innovations, and partnerships set it up to capture the promising opportunities ahead.

The IT major reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 12,380 crore in Q3, up 12 percent from year-ago period.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Moneycontrol pegged the IT services bellwether's Q3 net profit at Rs 12,308 crore and revenue at Rs 64,218 crore. The company's revenue rose 6 percent to Rs 63,973 crore.

Ahead of the result, the TCS share closed nearly 1.5 percent lower on BSE at Rs 4,046.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: IGL shares close 3% higher after GAIL raises domestic CNG allocation to 51%
data.description:
IGL

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Shares of Indraprastha Gas Limited (IGL) closed nearly 3% higher at Rs 418.70 apiece on January 9 after the firm announced that GAIL (India) Ltd. has decided to increase IGL’s domestic gas allocations in the CNG segment by 31%.

Consequently, IGL will have 51% domestic allocation in the CNG segment, higher than the current 37% level. The change will be effective from January 16 onwards, IGL informed in an exchange filing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The Company has also tied-up additional RLNG volumes on Term Basis at competitive prices, with one of the major suppliers (around 1.0 MMSCMD)," IGL said.

"The above revision and signing of additional volumes shall have a positive impact on profitability of the Company," it further added.

After the announcement, the stock had rallied around 7% from previous close to hit an intraday high of Rs 435.85. Later in the day, the stock pared some gains to fall to the closing levels. This coincides with the weak market sentiment, with Sensex closing 528 points lower at 77,620.21.

Notably, Maharashtra Natural Gas, a joint venture between BPCL, GAIL (India), and IGL, is preparing to list through an Initial Public Offering (IPO) of over Rs 1,000 crore. BPCL on January 6 announced that its board has given in-principle nod for the issue. International brokerage Citi Research said the listing could help unlock value for the firm. It reiterated its bullish 'buy' call on IGL shares, with a price target of Rs 450 per share.

data.title: Technical View: Support trendline breakout can bring selling pressure in Nifty 50, Bank Nifty closes at 7-month low
data.description:
Nifty Downtrend

The Nifty 50 maintained its downward journey and remained below the 200-day EMA (Exponential Moving Average) of 23,700 for another session on January 9 amid caution ahead of TCS earnings which were in line with analysts’ expectations. However, it continued to defend the upward sloping support trendline, which joins the previous November and December lows, coinciding with the 23,500 level. As long as the index holds this level, the chances of a rebound towards the 23,700-24,000 zone look high. However, a decisive fall below this level could drag the index down towards the November low of 23,263, experts said.

The Nifty 50 remained in negative territory throughout the session and finished at 23,527, down 162 points. It formed a bearish candlestick pattern on the daily charts with above-average volumes. The index sustained below all key moving averages, and the negative bias in the momentum indicators indicated overall weakness.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The zone of 23,550 – 23,500 will act as a make-or-break zone for the Nifty. A breach below this zone can lead to a decline towards the 23,263 level (November 2023 low), and below that, towards the psychological level of 23,000," said Jatin Gedia, Technical Research Analyst at Mirae Asset Sharekhan.

On the upside, according to him, the key hourly moving averages placed at 23,631 – 23,702 will act as immediate hurdles.

As per the weekly derivative data, the maximum Call open interest was seen at the 24,500 strike, followed by the 24,000 and 23,800 strikes, with maximum Call writing at the 24,500 strike, followed by the 23,800 and 24,000 strikes. On the Put side, the 23,000 strike holds the maximum open interest, followed by the 23,500 and 23,600 strikes, with maximum writing at the 23,000 strike, followed by the 23,600 and 22,700 strikes.

The above options data indicated that the index is likely to remain in the range of 23,000-24,000, with immediate support at 23,500 and resistance at 23,800.

Bank Nifty

The Bank Nifty also extended its downtrend for another session, correcting more than the benchmark Nifty 50. It fell by 332 points to 49,504, the lowest closing level since June 6, 2024. The index decisively closed below its August low and traded well below all key moving averages, while the 10-day EMA is on the verge of falling below the 200-day EMA, indicating bearish sentiment.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The banking index has seen a correction of 2,100 points since last Friday. "As long as it holds below the 49,750 zone, weakness could be seen towards 49,250, and then 48,750 levels. On the upside, resistance is seen at 49,750, followed by 50,000 levels," said Chandan Taparia, Senior Vice President and Head of Technical Research and Derivatives at Motilal Oswal Financial Services.

The India VIX, the volatility index, continued to maintain caution among bull traders, rising 1.33% to a level of 14.66. The VIX needs to sustain below the 14 mark for the bulls to regain strength.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

Tata Elxsi on Thursday reported a 3.6 percent decline in net profit to Rs 199 crore for the December 2024 quarter. It had posted a net profit of Rs 206.43 crore in the October-December period of the preceding 2023-24 fiscal, the company said in an exchange filing.

data.title: TCS headcount shrinks by 5,370 in Q3, reversing two quarters of growth
data.description:
Tata Consultancy Service

India’s largest IT services company Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) reported a net decline of 5,370 employees in its workforce during the fiscal third quarter, marking a reversal after two consecutive quarters of headcount growth.

The Tata group company's headcount had increased by 5,726 employees sequentially in the October quarter.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

With this decline, TCS now employs 6,07,354 people, the company said on January 9 as it shared the numbers for the third quarter of FY25.

“We promoted over 25,000 associates this quarter which brought the total promotions this financial year to more than 110,000. We continue to invest in employee upskilling and overall well-being. Our campus hiring for the year is going according to plan and preparations are afoot to onboard a higher number of campus hires next year," Chief Human Resources Officer Milind Lakkad was quoted as saying in the release.

Attrition for the quarter marginally rose to 13 percent, from the previous quarter’s 12.3 percent.

The Mumbai-based firm’s headcount fell for the first time in 19 years in FY24, marking a shift since the company listed on the bourses in 2004. The IT behemoth added 22,600 employees in FY23. The reversal is even steeper if one looks at FY22 numbers when it added a whopping net of 1.03 lakh employees.

For Q3, the IT major reported a consolidated net profit of Rs 12,380 crore, a rise of 12% compared to Rs 11,058 crore in the year-ago period.

Moneycontrol pegged the IT services bellwether's Q3 net profit at Rs 12,308 crore and revenue at Rs 64,218 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company's revenue rose 6% to Rs 63,973 crore in Q3FY25.

Ahead of the results, TCS shares on BSE fell nearly 1.5 percent today to end at Rs 4,046 apiece.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: BR Goyal Infrastructure SME IPO booked 82.77 times on final day; check GMP, allotment date
data.description:
BR Goyal Infrastructure IPO

BR Goyal Infrastructure's initial share sale continued to garner strong interest from investors, who have subscribed the SME IPO 82.77 times so far on the final day of bidding, i.e. January 9. The issue was opened for subscription on January 7.

The company that is engaged in the construction of infrastructure projects targets to raise Rs 85.21 crore through initial public offering which comprises of entirely fresh issue of 63.12 lakh shares. The price band for the offer is Rs 128-135 per share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Investors have subscribed for 37.46 crore equity shares against the issue size of 45.26 lakh shares via 1.61 lakh applications, as per the data available on the BSE.

Non-institutional investors were at the leading role to support the offer subscription, buying 132.98 times the allotted quota, followed by retail investors and qualified institutional buyers who bid 71.4 times and 54.31 times their reserved portion respectively. The part set aside for employees was also fully subscribed at 1.05 times.

The BR Goyal Infrastructure IPO shares were available at around 35-40 percent premium over the upper price band in the grey market (an unofficial platform for trading in IPO shares till the listing), the market observers said.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

The EPC company will finalise the share allotment by January 10, while the trading in its equity shares is set to commence on the BSE SME effective January 14.

BR Goyal that is currently executing road construction projects in Maharashtra, Gujarat, Mizoram, Manipur, and Uttar Pradesh has mobilised Rs 24.11 crore by issuing 17.86 lakh shares via anchor book at a price of Rs 135 per share on January 6.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

These IPO proceeds will be utilised mainly for its capital expenditure, working capital, and inorganic growth through acquisitions & other strategic initiatives.

Beeline Capital Advisors is acting as the merchant banker for the issue.
data.author: MoneyControl
